details: The agent ran comprehensive kubectl commands (describe, logs, get events, get svc/endpoints, get sc/pvc) against the actual cluster and correctly identified all 11 fault namespaces with accurate root-cause categories: fault-configmap (missing ConfigMap app-config), fault-crashloop (DB connection refused on startup), fault-endpoints (service selector mismatch: web-backend-v2 vs web-backend labels), fault-imagepull (nonexistent image tag), fault-init (init container failing due to migration table lock), fault-multicontainer (sidecar DNS resolution failure for log-collector.logging.svc), fault-oom (OOMKilled due to tail /dev/zero exceeding 25Mi memory limit), fault-pvc (PVC pending due to nonexistent StorageClass fast-ssd), fault-readiness (readiness probe targeting port 8080 but nginx listens on 80), fault-rollout (deployment stuck with ProgressDeadlineExceeded due to invalid image), fault-unschedulable (node selector disktype=ssd-nvme-gen5 matches no nodes). The healthy namespace was not flagged. All diagnoses are directly supported by the command output with no hallucination.
